signals.headline: South Korea's March 2026 annual inflation rises to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea's March 2026 annual inflation rises to 2.2%, below forecasts of 2.4% | YES | HIGH confidence**  
This is the latest confirmed inflation data (released recently per source timing), up from 2.0% in Jan-Feb but milder than expected, signaling contained pressures amid Middle East energy volatility; Bank of Korea held rates at 2.5% last week and raised 2026 inflation outlook to 2.2% from 2.
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: No breaking Germany inflation, GDP, or debt announcements confirmed today | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
No breaking Germany inflation, GDP, or debt announcements confirmed today | NO | HIGH confidence**  
Recent IMF revisions lowered Germany's debt-to-GDP projection slightly to 73.6% (from 74.8%), with OECD forecasting 0.3% GDP growth in 2025 and 1% in 2026, but nothing "just" resolved or announced to newly move markets.[2][7]

signals.headline: Trump Reiterates Demands for Iran to Remove Nuclear Stockpile, Hints at Resumed Talks Despite Red Line Conflicts | NO |
signals.raw:
Trump Reiterates Demands for Iran to Remove Nuclear Stockpile, Hints at Resumed Talks Despite Red Line Conflicts | NO | HIGH confidence**  
Trump insists on no deal allowing Iran to retain enriched uranium, viewing current negotiations as likely to fail due to irreconcilable red lines, though he hints at resuming talks after Pakistan collapse.[1][2]
